search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk/guidance/ng12 pigmented skin lesion change size shape colour
site:nice.org.uk/guidance/ng14 pigmented lesion photography dermoscopy follow-up
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic melanoma diagnosis, biopsy, excision, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The legacy workflow does not contain all score inputs and no score was calculated.
All diagnosis and management decisions remain clinician-entered.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with a pigmented lesion under review; children, pregnancy, previous melanoma, familial syndromes, and immunosuppression need specialist context.
setting_applicability: UAE primary and specialist dermatology or skin-cancer assessment.
UAE_applicability: Recognition and documentation fields are transferable, but UAE cancer, dermoscopy, photography, pathology, consent, and referral pathways remain required.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG12 updated in 2026 and NICE NG14 updated in 2022 were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The reviewed NICE NG12 and NG14 recommendations are current.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact skin-cancer recognition section supports documenting a changing pigmented lesion, interval change, symptoms, clinician skin inspection, distribution or site, inflammation, oozing and sensation change without asserting malignancy or generating referral.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists suspicious pigmented-lesion features including size change, irregular shape or colour, diameter, inflammation, oozing and sensation change, plus dermoscopy and referral contexts for melanoma, squamous-cell carcinoma, and basal-cell carcinoma.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact melanoma-assessment section supports documenting clinical photographs reviewed when available and consented, and a clinician-completed lesion assessment; it does not create dermoscopic findings or diagnosis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording trained-clinician dermoscopy, baseline clinical or dermoscopic photography, and interval comparison for a clinically atypical melanocytic lesion in specialist care.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact safety-netting section supports recording clinician-discussed safety net and arranged follow-up only.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact site, dimensions, morphology, dermoscopy, skin type, sun exposure, family and personal cancer history, prior images, diagnosis, biopsy or excision decision, pathology, referral, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
